answer.text: Regulate, promote and ensure orderly growth of the insurance sector

explanation.text: **The Logic:** IRDAI (Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India) regulates and promotes orderly growth of the insurance sector, including re-insurance, while protecting policyholders' interests. **The Trap:** Don't confuse IRDAI with PFRDA — IRDAI handles insurance while PFRDA handles pensions. Annuities sold under NPS are insurance products regulated by IRDAI. **Cross-Exam Context:** IRDAI's role appears in Series X-B (insurance module) and is relevant for Series XVII because NPS annuities are provided by IRDAI-regulated insurance companies. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Annuity rates and products from NPS exit are regulated by IRDAI, not PFRDA. When comparing annuity options, check IRDAI-empanelled ASPs.
